title: Margin & padding shorthand
description: One line, four values, easy to fumble.

questions.explanation: Four values run clockwise: top, right, bottom, left.
Three values = top, horizontal (both), bottom. So right = 8px.

questions.prompt: `margin: 0 auto;` centers the element horizontally IF:
options: It has display: block and a defined width
questions.explanation: Block-level + explicit width → auto splits leftover space equally.

questions.prompt: What is a NEGATIVE margin useful for?
options: Pulling an element outside its parent
questions.explanation: Negative margins have many legitimate uses — carefully.
Two values: vertical then horizontal. Bottom = vertical = 8px.

questions.prompt: Which is a modern spacing property that avoids per-side syntax?
questions.explanation: gap sets space BETWEEN items without needing per-item margins.

options: Respect writing direction — 'start' means left in LTR, right in RTL
Same as margin-left in all cases
questions.explanation: Great for supporting both LTR and RTL languages without duplicating styles.
